instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `define-memoized` that defines a function with a private cache hash-table. Repeated calls with equal argument lists return the cached value. The cache, the lookup key, and the multiple-value-bind temporaries must all be hidden behind gensyms so they cannot clash with user code in body.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 (MAKE-HASH-TABLE :TEST 'EQUAL)))
            (DEFUN SLOW-FN (N)
              (LET ((#:G2 (LIST N)))
                (MULTIPLE-VALUE-BIND (#:G3 #:G4)
                    (GETHASH #:G2 #:G1)
                  (IF #:G4
                      #:G3
                      (SETF (GETHASH #:G2 #:G1) (PROGN (* N N))))))))
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 (MAKE-HASH-TABLE :TEST 'EQUAL)))
            (DEFUN SLOW-FN (N)
              (LET ((#:G113 (LIST N)))
                (MULTIPLE-VALUE-BIND (#:G114 #:G115)
                    (GETHASH #:G113 #:G112)
                  (IF #:G115
